findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Blocker
findings.description: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch, which is a critical blocker for the EU market. Key concerns include lawful basis for processing, consent mechanisms, data retention policies, right to erasure, and completion of the DPIA.
assumptions: The product will be launched in the EU market.
recommendations: Perform a full GDPR compliance audit to address concerns.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit.

findings.title: Privacy Risks in User Tracking
findings.description: There are significant privacy risks associated with the user behavior tracking pipeline and cross-device tracking. Legal has flagged potential issues with user consent and data anonymization, which could lead to compliance violations.
assumptions: User consent mechanisms are not fully established.
recommendations: Establish clear user consent mechanisms and ensure data anonymization.

findings.title: Lack of Tiered Consent Mechanism
findings.description: PersonaLens currently lacks a tiered consent mechanism similar to ClearFeed, which allows users to choose their level of data sharing. This could hinder user trust and adoption, especially among privacy-conscious consumers.
assumptions: Users prefer more control over their data sharing.
recommendations: Implement a tiered consent mechanism to enhance user trust.

findings.title: Regulatory Compliance Risks
findings.description: PersonaLens must ensure compliance with GDPR regulations to avoid potential fines similar to those faced by DataPulse. The lack of a defined consent mechanism and data retention policy poses a significant risk.
assumptions: The product will be subject to GDPR regulations.
recommendations: Define a clear consent mechanism and establish data retention policies.

metadata.mitigation: Define a clear consent mechanism and establish data retention policies.

findings.title: Authentication Weaknesses
findings.description: Detected authentication risk keywords indicate potential weaknesses in the authentication flow, which could lead to unauthorized access to user data.
assumptions: Current authentication mechanisms may not be robust enough.
recommendations: Review and strengthen authentication mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access.

summary: Identified 5 risks (2 compliance, 2 privacy, 1 security). 1 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (2 blockers, 2 warnings).
